LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. BART HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE TO SHOW THAT THE DANGEROUS CONDITION WAS NOT DANGEROUS AS A MATTER OF LAW. IN THE COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF ALLEGED TWO DANGEROUS CONDITIONS PERTAINING TO THE NON-SKID MATERIAL AND POOR LIGHTING CONDITIONS. BART HAS NOT ADDRESSED THE ALLEGATIONS OF POOR LIGHTING CONDITIONS. BART'S SAFETY RATE IS PREMISED ON AN INCORRECT NUMBER OF PATRONS. (RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS, P. 2:3-14. SEE UMF NO. 25.) THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING NOTICE OF A DANGEROUS CONDITION BASED ON THE 12/02/08 CLAIM AND WHETHER THIS CLAIM IS SUBSTANTIALLY SIMILAR TO THE SUBJECT INCIDENT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION CANNOT LIE AS TO PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. (CCP 437C(F)(1).) PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO ROSSEN DECL., P. 1:28 - 2:1 IS SUSTAINED. BART'S OBJECTION NOS. 1 - 5, 7 ARE SUSTAINED. BART'S OBJECTION NO. 6 IS OVERRULED. BART'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MUNI HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE TO SHOW THAT THE DANGEROUS CONDITION WAS NOT DANGEROUS AS A MATTER OF LAW. IN THE COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF ALLEGED TWO DANGEROUS CONDITIONS PERTAINING TO THE NON-SKID MATERIAL AND POOR LIGHTING CONDITIONS. MUNI HAS NOT ADDRESSED THE ALLEGATIONS OF POOR LIGHTING CONDITIONS. BART'S SAFETY RATE IS PREMISED ON AN INCORRECT NUMBER. (SEE UMF NO. 24.) THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING NOTICE OF A DANGEROUS CONDITION BASED ON THE 12/02/08 CLAIM AND WHETHER THIS CLAIM IS SUBSTANTIALLY SIMILAR TO THE SUBJECT INCIDENT. ALTHOUGH THE 12/02/08 CLAIM WAS FILED WITH BART, BART AND MUNI ARE REQUIRED TO EQUALLY SHARE THE INVESTIGATION OF ALL THIRD PARTY CLAIMS PURSUANT TO THE JOINT STATION MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT. (PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE, EXHIBIT G, P. 13 - 14.) PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO HAMMOND DECL., P. 2:10-12 IS SUSTAINED, AND PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO HALL DECL., P. 2:8-13 AND 2:14-15 ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT ANDREW BONZELL, TEL. NO. (510) 625-7700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KIMBERLY Y. CHIN & ATTY. MARK HAZELWOOD OF LOW BALL & LYNCH, TEL. NO. 394-3661 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BART.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or in the alternative summary adjudication is Denied as to summary judgment and summary adjudication. BART has not met its initial burden to demonstrate to show that the dangerous condition was not dangerous as a matter of law. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleged two dangerous conditions pertaining to the non-skid material and poor lighting conditions. BART has not addressed the allegations of poor lighting conditions. BART's safety rate is premised on an incorrect number of patrons. (Response to Plaintiff's Objections, p. 2:3-14. See UMF no. 25.) There are triable issues of fact regarding notice of a dangerous condition based on the 12/02/08 claim and whether this claim is substantially similar to the subject incident. Summary adjudication cannot lie as to part of a cause of action. (CCP 437c(f)(1).) Plaintiff's objection to Rossen Decl., p. 1:28 - 2:1 is sustained. BART's Objection nos. 1 - 5, 7 are sustained. BART's Objection no. 6 is overruled. BART's request for judicial notice is granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
=(302/EHG)

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. MUNI has not met its initial burden to demonstrate to show that the dangerous condition was not dangerous as a matter of law. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleged two dangerous conditions pertaining to the non-skid material and poor lighting conditions. MUNI has not addressed the allegations of poor lighting conditions. BART's safety rate is premised on an incorrect number. (See UMF no. 24.) There are triable issues of fact regarding notice of a dangerous condition based on the 12/02/08 claim and whether this claim is substantially similar to the subject incident. Although the 12/02/08 claim was filed with BART, BART and MUNI are required to equally share the investigation of all third party claims pursuant to the Joint Station Maintenance Agreement. (Plaintiff's Evidence, Exhibit G, p. 13 - 14.) Plaintiff's objection to Hammond Decl., p. 2:10-12 is sustained, and Plaintiff's objections to Hall Decl., p. 2:8-13 and 2:14-15 are overruled.
